title: Save conditional coloring to main table
excerpt: I started using the conditional coloring but it only works if you save the view. And unfortunately what this does is to create a new table. Keeping the main table with no conditional coloring feature. Which is a broken l…

posts.author: FloPaduraru (Florin Paduraru)
posts.cookedHtml:
I started using the conditional coloring but it only works if you save the view. And unfortunately what this does is to create a new table. Keeping the main table with no conditional coloring feature. Which is a broken logic.
It would be great if we could save conditional coloring on the main table and not being forced to duplicate…

posts.author: Pray59 (Patti Ray)
posts.cookedHtml: Looking for the same thing. My dashboards won’t reflect the color coding if it isn’t on the main table.

posts.author: simone.steffen (Simone Steffen)
posts.cookedHtml: There’s another feature request for this here, if you want to vote for it as well: Conditional Coloring in Main View

posts.author: ClaireM-HD (Claire Miller)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi team. We have recently started using conditional colouring on one of our boards to highlight some key details where needed. I was just wondering if there are any improvements coming through the pipeline soon that would allow for conditional colouring to be saved to the main board, instead of having to save it to a new view. Sometimes our team members views get changed from the default board (w/ conditional colouring) to the ‘main board’ and it causes confusion when the main board no longer looks the same as our default view/board. It’s tricky having to have multiple board views for different scenarios, when the easiest thing would be to be able to save filters/sorts/conditional colouring/etc. to the main board itself. I can provide a more clear picture of our specific use-case if that will help. Thanks so much!

posts.author: elcloche (Alejandro Graffe)
posts.cookedHtml:
Please!!! this is a must!

We need more votes!

Please vote people!!

posts.author: DataSteg (Chad Stegman)
posts.cookedHtml: I honestly thought it was a bug that the conditional formatting wasn’t saving to the Main Table. Why do I need to create an entirely new view just to be able to save conditional formatting, especially when considering that my board only has a single view? This is a bit redundant on monday.com’s part.

posts.author: BrendanHarris (Brendan Harris)
posts.cookedHtml: Agreed, this is another one of those Monday idiosyncrasies that if fixed, would help to reduce the clutter. Just upvoted.

posts.author: KaterinaR (Katerina Rudkovskaya)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello,

I’m Katerina from Stiltsoft. Please join our discussion here: How do you work with tables in monday?
We’ll be very glad to know more about your use cases and needs.

posts.author: bdelin (Bryce Delin)
posts.cookedHtml: There’s no reason it shouldn’t work this way out of the box. This is silly.

posts.author: kkovriga (Katerina Kovriga)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi guys,

Our company develops the Smart Spreadsheet for monday app - hope it will help you to use Excel-like features right on monday.com platform.

You’ll be able to import/export your data (and boards with items as well) and work with it as a fully functional spreadsheet (cell formulas, conditional formatting, grouped tables, etc.) in one place.

Best regards,
Katerina Kovriga

posts.author: kaylak (Kayla K)
posts.cookedHtml: I thought I was missing a save button or this was a bug as well. Being able to save to the main table view would be incredibly helpful for our team rather than creating another view which can clutter our boards.

posts.author: JMunoz (Jairo)
posts.cookedHtml: Same here! I thought it was a bug because of the weird behavior.

posts.author: farrellb (Brandon Farrell)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes, this would be a great feature.

posts.author: Julia1 (Julia)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi all,
I was looking for something similar as well and came across this post. Do you know if there is any third party app that could help with that?
Thank you

posts.author: christinap (Christina Punchard)
posts.cookedHtml: It’s Aug 2023 and it’s still an issue. Anyone found a workaround yet?

posts.author: imoll2611 (Igor Moll)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is a no-brainer and should be a prime function in every ‘spreadsheet’ based tool.

I am not getting Monday’s idea of creating all these different views of the same table. WHY???
I just want one main table and additional (that is really great) views showing me a dashboard etc…

The same works for the filters and sorting function (columns): why can you not tweak that in a way you want it, save it to the main board and have it available there? Tip: look at how SmartSheet tables work wit the filters, conditional formatting etc… Seems like Monday can learn something there…

Pls have this fixed asap.

posts.author: simood (Simon O'Donnell)
posts.cookedHtml:
Agreed. Seems silly to have to create an unnecessary extra view.
Please make this update Monday Team.

posts.author: ian_suckling (ian suckling)
posts.cookedHtml:
Agree, we have several staff with their own formatted view of the same table. We want to set conditional coloring to Main Table (once!) and have it reflected in all staffs personalised view of the table. As it stands any updates to main table conditions need to be manauly added to the staff views in the 1st instance and then repeated every time the Main Table conditions are updated.

This does appear to be lacking a very useful feature that is a fair requirement, I’m sue these Forum posts are just the tip of the iceberg off Monday.Com users with these frustrations.

Can Monday.Com at least make a comment here please

posts.author: DrGerry (Gerald)
posts.cookedHtml: It’s been almost 2 years since this request was made. Is anyone at Monday.com even paying attention?

posts.author: trinja (Trinity Doyle)
posts.cookedHtml: This should absolutely happen. We have team members that will only look at certain boards in dashboard views and as that comes from the main board the conditional colouring doesn’t carry across.

posts.author: rasarch (Rachel Story)
posts.cookedHtml:
Adding to the voices asking for this one.

Also need the conditional formatting to be able to apply to the whole board at once instead of each column separately - it’s a pain to reformat every column separately just to get all the numbers less than zero to show red.

posts.author: STNSteph (Steph Diaz)
posts.cookedHtml: Adding to the conversation that this feature would also be helpful for my team.

posts.author: jrhams (Jeff)
posts.cookedHtml: Up vote. You should be able to make changes to the Main Table or, if not, at least hide the Main Table.

posts.author: Doradocsvs (Jennifer Raynow)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes. Putting in my vote here! This feature would be incredibly helpful for my team as well!!

posts.author: EricBarker (Eric Barker)
posts.cookedHtml: My team relies a lot on color, and consistency throughout views. Conditional Coloring feels very gimped to us, as you can’t keep it consistent across views, and can’t apply it to the Main Table view. I basically want to be able to turn Formulas into a brightly colored Status column indicator, and that’s not possible. Same with Multi-select dropdowns.

posts.author: pgraunke (Pam Graunke)
posts.cookedHtml: I’ve been working for days trying to keep the conditional colors on the Main Board. I wish I had known ahead of time. I definitely need this functionality.

posts.author: JonathanUttley (Jonathan Uttley)
posts.cookedHtml: Any update on this?

posts.author: jonathan.01 (jonathan)
posts.cookedHtml:
I used “save as a new view” and then renamed to “main table” and “set as board default”
which seems to do it. or am I missing something?

posts.author: hrswick (Holly Swick)
posts.cookedHtml:
I agree completely. We avoid creating extra views for client-facing boards to minimize confusion. If it were possible to hide the main view, that would also solve this issue for us too.

If you haven’t already, please upvote for this feature, so the monday product team will prioritize it.

posts.author: amybronstein (Amy Bronstein)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone! Amy here from the monday.com team Thank you so much for your feedback around the need for this feature, we hear you!

At this time, this feature isn’t on our long-term roadmap, as we’re currently focused on other high-impact priorities that are further along in development. That said, we know this is important to lots of our community, so we’ll continue to evaluate it as we plan ahead.

We’re always listening, and we’ll make sure to share any updates here if anything changes!

posts.author: si_novum (Simon Kemp)
posts.cookedHtml: This would be really useful!

posts.author: shannon-cxmonday (Shannon Thao)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello everyone! We really appreciate your continued feedback. We will not be prioritizing this feature on our long-term roadmap. The Main Table on a board serves as the database. Therefore, we intend to maintain the Main Table as a “clean” view for users to reference. Any custom visualization edits or filters can be saved as a new board view, as many users have referenced in this thread.

We encourage you to upvote this existing feature request, “Hide option for Main Table View,” which may address this feature request. It is under strong consideration for the second half of this year. Thank you all for being a part of optimizing the monday.com platform!

posts.author: Tom-CXmonday (Tom)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello all, Tom from monday here

I wanted to share here about a new feature that we started releasing gradually, which allows you to hide your Main Table:

image611×398 46.8 KB

While it doesn’t allow saving conditional coloring or filters to the main view, it does allow hiding it, and with the ability to unpin the main table and pin other views on the board, you can achieve a similar goal to the one described in this discussion

I hope you find this information useful! To learn more about this new release: https://support.monday.com/hc/en-us/articles/360001267945-The-board-views
